message:
Address RFC 6587 octet-counting review findings

- Add 16MB maximum message size limit for octet-counting (SEC-1 security hardening)
- Rename iterator variable from `it` to `octet_it` for clarity (RDY-1)
- Improve comments explaining the three octet-counting modes (RDY-3)
- Rename test file octet_counting.tql to octet_counting_auto.tql (RDY-6)
- Add comprehensive edge case test coverage:
  - octet_counting_length_mismatch.tql (TST-1, TST-6, TST-7)
  - octet_counting_false_with_prefix.tql (TST-1, TST-6, TST-7)
  - octet_counting_zero_length.tql (TST-1, TST-6, TST-7)
- Factor max_syslog_message_size constant to namespace scope for reuse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+// octet_counting=false should NOT strip numeric prefix
+// Input starting with digits should be parsed as-is (will fail syslog parse)
+from { x: "65 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=false)

@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+// Auto-detect with mismatched length prefix (10 != actual 65)
+// Should fall back to regular syslog parsing and fail (numeric prefix not valid syslog)
+from { x: "10 <165>1 2023-10-11T22:14:15.003Z host app 1234 ID01 - Test message" }
+x = x.parse_syslog()

@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+// Zero-length message prefix (RFC 6587 requires NONZERO-DIGIT)
+from { x: "0 " }
+x = x.parse_syslog(octet_counting=true)
